reason: The remediation modifies the mount options of the /boot partition, which is a boot filesystem change. Policy dictates that partitioning/filesystem/bootloader modifications be classified as too_dangerous_to_remediate due to the risk of rendering the system unbootable if applied incorrectly.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure if fstab entry is malformed; May prevent execution of required setuid binaries during early boot (unlikely but possible); Requires reboot to apply, increasing downtime risk
